name: criminal_procedure_communications_outage_cedar_lib_eq
premise: Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. A cross-functional incident desk is managing a domain incident with incomplete information and high reputational exposure. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: criminal procedure communications outage cedar lib eq. The moral tension is explicitly framed as liberty versus equality: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. A whistleblower tip has reached a reporter; silence reads as cover-up, while a rushed statement reads as recklessness. A wire cutoff and settlement window closes in under thirty minutes; reversals may be impossible until the next business day. Preserve chain-of-custody for evidence; no bulk deletion or retention-policy changes during the incident. Treat unverified telemetry as hypothesis; do not assert facts you cannot support from retained logs.

agents.name: Elliot Bennett
agents.identity: I am Elliot Bennett, the accountable decision-maker on this Criminal Procedure bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes equality whenever someone invokes liberty.

agents.name: Marco Kaur
agents.identity: I am Marco Kaur, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so equality is not sacrificed by default just because liberty feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect equality.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to equality to secure liberty before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: External parties will infer a decision if none is posted. With partial evidence, Elliot Bennett must decide under liberty versus equality. What does Elliot Bennett do?
